event: Virgin of the Milk enthroned between Saint Peter and Saint Francis
symbols: Nursing infant — the full humanity of the Incarnation, God nourished by a human mother
Gold background — the eternal heavenly realm
Peter with book — the apostolic foundation of the Church, keeper of the keys
Francis in brown habit with book — poverty and devotion to Christ crucified
Elaborate halo and throne — Mary as Queen of Heaven, Sedes Sapientiae
description: The Virgin Mary sits enthroned on a red and gold cloth against a gold background, wearing a deep blue mantle over a red gown, with an ornate punched-gold halo. She holds the infant Jesus who nurses at her breast. To the left, a bearded man with a halo in yellow and green robes holds a red book — Saint Peter. To the right, a smaller tonsured figure in a dark Franciscan habit holds a red book — Saint Francis. The panel has an arched top and the gold background is decorated with incised patterns.
theological_description: This altarpiece presents the Virgin and Child in the tradition of the Maria Lactans (Nursing Madonna), flanked by Saint Peter and Saint Francis in a Sacra Conversazione. The nursing motif affirms the full reality of the Incarnation — the eternal Word truly took human flesh and was nourished by a human mother (GAL.4.4). Peter's presence on the left represents the apostolic foundation: 'You are Peter, and on this rock I will build my church' (MAT.16.18). Francis, though not a biblical figure, embodies the radical imitation of Christ in poverty, bearing the stigmata in his body. The gold background is the visual language of eternity — these figures exist outside temporal space, in the communion of saints before the throne of God. The composition declares that the Incarnation is the center around which all Christian witness — apostolic and monastic — revolves.

scripture_refs.reason: God sent His Son, born of a woman — the Incarnation embodied in the nursing Madonna

The virgin shall conceive and bear a son — the prophetic foundation of the Madonna and Child
